description: Per-room ESP32-C5 WiFi probe fleet: fleet verdict strip, reachability SLA (gateway/DNS/internet/HTTPS), link quality (RSSI/channel/BSSID), device health, and per-room AP survey. Rooms come from the scrape-time `room` label. Generated by scripts/grafana/build_wifi_probes_overview.py.

panels.description: Fraction of wifi-probe scrape targets Prometheus can reach (band-independent up{}). or vector(0) keeps an empty/no-target fleet at a clear 0 instead of No data.
panels.title: 📡 Probes Up

panels.description: Distinct rooms with at least one live probe right now. Green at ≥1 so a single-room (or $room-filtered) deployment isn't falsely degraded.
panels.title: 🏠 Rooms Covered

panels.description: internet_https (generate_204) reachability of the weakest room. max-by-room = a room is OK if EITHER band reached the internet (avoids a false FAIL from the stale off-band); outer min = FAIL only if some room has no working band.
panels.title: 🌐 Worst-Room Internet

panels.description: Most negative connected-link RSSI across selected rooms/bands.
panels.title: 📶 Weakest Link RSSI

panels.description: Total WiFi disconnects over $window (includes expected band-switch flaps).
panels.title: 🔌 Disconnects ($window)

panels.description: Lowest free heap among selected probes — closest-to-OOM device.
panels.title: 🧠 Min Free Heap

panels.description: Oldest probe last-success age. NOTE: a never-succeeded probe emits no age series; pair with Probes Failing.
panels.title: ⏱️ Max Probe Staleness

panels.description: Count of room×probe×band checks whose last attempt failed. or vector(0) keeps a healthy 0 green.
panels.title: ❌ Probes Failing
🎯 Probe Results — reachability SLA (per room × probe)

panels.description: Share of room×probe×band checks whose last attempt succeeded. A fraction (not a raw count) so the verdict stays correct when you filter $room or $band.

targets.expr: sum(probe_success{job="wifi-probe", room=~"$room", band=~"$band"}) / count(probe_success{job="wifi-probe", room=~"$room", band=~"$band"})

panels.title: ✅ Checks passing %

panels.description: Single worst room×probe success ratio over $window. clamp_min avoids 0/0; prefer $window ≥15m.

targets.expr: min(sum(increase(probe_success_total{job="wifi-probe", room=~"$room", band=~"$band"}[$window])) by (room, probe) / clamp_min(sum(increase(probe_attempts_total{job="wifi-probe", room=~"$room", band=~"$band"}[$window])) by (room, probe), 1))

panels.title: 📉 Worst success ratio

panels.description: Oldest last-success across room×probe. Never-succeeded probes are omitted here — see the matrix.
panels.title: ⏱️ Stalest probe age

panels.description: Worst internet_https code. 204 = clean internet; 200/302 = captive portal / DNS hijack; 0 = timeout.
panels.title: 🌐 HTTPS status (204?)

panels.description: THE core SLA view: OK/FAIL lane per room×probe×band over time. spanNulls holds value across the off-band phase. Per-band lanes expose a band-specific dead spot.
panels.title: 🎯 Probe success matrix — room × probe × band

panels.description: Δsuccess/Δattempts over $window, bands merged per room×probe. Generous window smooths the per-cycle quantization; clamp_min avoids 0/0 gaps.

targets.expr: sum(increase(probe_success_total{job="wifi-probe", room=~"$room", band=~"$band"}[$window])) by (room, probe) / clamp_min(sum(increase(probe_attempts_total{job="wifi-probe", room=~"$room", band=~"$band"}[$window])) by (room, probe), 1)

panels.title: Success ratio over $window (room × probe)

panels.description: probe_last_duration_seconds per room×probe×band. Coarse staircase (one step/cycle); flat held segments between cycles are normal.
panels.title: Live probe latency — last attempt

panels.description: Δsum/Δcount windowed mean (the only panel over duration_sum/_count). Bands merged; clamp_min guards empty windows.

targets.expr: sum(increase(probe_duration_seconds_sum{job="wifi-probe", room=~"$room", band=~"$band"}[$window])) by (room, probe) / clamp_min(sum(increase(probe_duration_seconds_count{job="wifi-probe", room=~"$room", band=~"$band"}[$window])) by (room, probe), 1)

panels.title: Avg probe latency over $window

panels.description: internet_https code per room/band — a flip 204→200/302 marks a captive portal; 0 marks a hard timeout.
panels.title: HTTPS status code over time

panels.description: Dense per-(room,probe,band) sheet joining every probe metric. A blank Success-age next to Up=0 is the silently-failing / never-succeeded signature. target column confirms each probe is aimed correctly.

targets.expr: sum(increase(probe_success_total{job="wifi-probe", room=~"$room", band=~"$band"}[$window])) by (room, probe, band, target, type, instance) / clamp_min(sum(increase(probe_attempts_total{job="wifi-probe", room=~"$room", band=~"$band"}[$window])) by (room, probe, band, target, type, instance), 1)

sum(increase(probe_duration_seconds_sum{job="wifi-probe", room=~"$room", band=~"$band"}[$window])) by (room, probe, band, target, type, instance) / clamp_min(sum(increase(probe_duration_seconds_count{job="wifi-probe", room=~"$room", band=~"$band"}[$window])) by (room, probe, band, target, type, instance), 1)

panels.title: 📋 Probe SLA matrix — room × probe × band
Probe error stage — why a probe fails (room × probe × band)
panels.description: Failing stage of the last attempt per probe slot. OK/green when passing; otherwise names the layer that broke: DNS resolution, TCP connect, TLS handshake/cert, HTTP response, timeout, or an internal/local error. Firmware >= 0.5.1.2.

panels.title: Probe failures by stage over $window
panels.description: increase(probe_errors_total) grouped by room, probe and failing stage. Flat zero = healthy. Firmware >= 0.5.1.2.

targets.expr: sum by (room, probe, stage) (increase(probe_errors_total{job="wifi-probe", room=~"$room", band=~"$band"}[$window]))

panels.description: Ranks every room×probe by staleness. CAVEAT: a never-succeeded probe shows NO bar — cross-check the success matrix (always has a lane).
panels.title: Probe freshness — last-success age
📶 WiFi Link Quality — RSSI / channel / BSSID

panels.description: Sparse staircase: each band refreshes ~once/cycle and is absent during the other band's phase. stepBefore + spanNulls hold the last value; points mark real samples.
panels.title: RSSI by room & band

panels.description: last_over_time over $window so a band that's briefly off-air keeps its last reading instead of dropping out.
panels.title: Current RSSI per room (band-split)

panels.description: RSSI + channel + BSSID/SSID/auth per room×band. group_left carries the AP labels onto each value so the frames share one label set; last_over_time keeps a briefly off-air band populated.

targets.expr: last_over_time(wifi_client_rssi_dbm{job="wifi-probe", room=~"$room", band=~"$band"}[$window]) * on (instance, band) group_left(bssid, ssid, auth) (topk(1, last_over_time(wifi_client_bssid_info{job="wifi-probe", room=~"$room", band=~"$band"}[$window])) by (instance, band))

last_over_time(wifi_client_channel{job="wifi-probe", room=~"$room", band=~"$band"}[$window]) * on (instance, band) group_left(bssid, ssid, auth) (topk(1, last_over_time(wifi_client_bssid_info{job="wifi-probe", room=~"$room", band=~"$band"}[$window])) by (instance, band))

panels.title: Current link detail per room

panels.description: Channel as labeled segments per room/band — spot 5g DFS moves or 2g 1/6/11 hops.
panels.title: Link channel over time (roams)

panels.description: One row per probe: build_info labels (incl. firmware self-reported location) joined with uptime, free heap, and live connected flag. Compare `Self-reported room` to `Room` (scrape label) to confirm a probe is physically where its target claims.

targets.expr: max by (room, instance) (wifi_probe_uptime_seconds{job="wifi-probe", room=~"$room"}) * on (instance) group_left(version, idf, chip, location) wifi_probe_build_info{job="wifi-probe", room=~"$room"}
max by (room, instance) (wifi_probe_heap_free_bytes{job="wifi-probe", room=~"$room"}) * on (instance) group_left(version, idf, chip, location) wifi_probe_build_info{job="wifi-probe", room=~"$room"}
max by (room, instance) (wifi_client_connected{job="wifi-probe", room=~"$room"}) * on (instance) group_left(version, idf, chip, location) wifi_probe_build_info{job="wifi-probe", room=~"$room"}

panels.title: 🏷️ Probe identity & build — per room

panels.description: Sawtooth that drops to ~0 marks a reboot/crash. Device-level (not band-gated).
panels.title: ⏱️ Uptime since boot — per room

panels.description: Downward drift over hours = leak → predicts an OOM reboot. Second series is the per-$window low-water mark.
panels.title: 🧠 Free heap — leak watch

panels.description: increase() over the generous $window (band-alternating counter only advances ~once/cycle). No band label on this counter.
panels.title: 🔌 Disconnects — increase/$window

panels.description: One lane per room. max by(room,instance) suppresses cosmetic single-scrape dips on band switch; a sustained red band is a real outage.
panels.title: 📶 Connected state — band-switch flaps

panels.description: Passive-scan AP list per room×band×BSSID. The same BSSID at different RSSI from different rooms is your coverage map. Top ~6 APs per band; an empty table = scan not yet populated for that band.
panels.title: 📡 Surveyed APs per room (RSSI desc)

panels.description: Strongest AP each room can hear, per band — the single-number coverage verdict. A red bar flags a dead-spot room; an absent bar = no AP heard on that band.
panels.title: Best AP RSSI per room (strongest seen)

panels.description: Coverage drift / interference fades. Stair-stepped due to per-cycle scan refresh; do not rate()-smooth a gauge.
panels.title: Best AP RSSI per room — trend

panels.description: Co-channel congestion: distinct APs seen per channel/band. Inner max-by dedupes a BSSID seen from several rooms. LOWER is better (opposite polarity to RSSI). Undercounts (top-6 cap).

targets.expr: count by (channel, band) (max by (channel, band, bssid) (wifi_ap_rssi_dbm{job="wifi-probe", room=~"$room", band=~"$band"}))

panels.title: Channel occupancy — distinct BSSIDs/channel

panels.description: Distinct radios (BSSIDs), networks (SSIDs), and channels in the current room/band scan.
panels.title: 📻 Airspace inventory
📡 AP Survey — what each room sees (passive scan)

title: 📡 WiFi Probes — Household Coverage
